rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Protective Order Re Additional Time To Depose Pltf Michael Kelly; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 13, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1, Defendants Rheem Manufacturing Company, E.A. Hathaway & Company, and Hill Brothers Chemical Company¿s Motion for Protective Order Regarding Additional Time to Depose Plaintiff Michael Kelly is granted. Defendants showed good cause for additional time. The deposition will be extended an additional 15 hours beyond the General Order 129 limit. Both plaintiffs¿ and defendants¿ requests for sanctions are denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring Defendant John K. Bice Company Inc. To Verify Its Written Discovery Responses And Deeming Plaintiffs' Requests For Admission Admitted; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 13, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel verified responses to written discovery from defendant John K. Bice Co., Inc. and to deem requests for admission admitted is denied. Defendant, a dissolved corporation, is under no obligation to provide verifications. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) Since defendant is not required to provide verifications, defendant¿s timely answers to plaintiffs¿ requests for admission will not be deemed admitted. Both plaintiffs¿ and defendant¿s requests for monetary sanctions are denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
